era: 1981-present. Progressive Metal. E=8 -- precision, long-form composition, concept album architecture. Bellevue, Eastside. EMI Records.

theory_nodes.concept: Operation: Mindcrime (1988) -- first concept album in engine. 15 tracks, one narrative: Nikki / Dr. X / Sister Mary. Political allegory: cult recruitment, ideological manipulation, betrayal. Geoff Tate five-octave vocal range. Chris DeGarmo long-form composition. One of the greatest concept albums in metal history.
theory_nodes.description: The engine's first concept album entry. Northwest progressive metal at full compositional ambition.

theory_nodes.concept: Empire (1990) -- most commercially successful metal album by a Pacific Northwest band. 3 million US copies. 'Silent Lucidity' Billboard Hot 100 #9, Grammy nomination, MTV rotation. Major label (EMI) resource deployment in service of mainstream metal crossover.
theory_nodes.description: The commercial peak of Northwest metal, documented before the grunge moment.

theory_nodes.concept: Bellevue Eastside origin: organized, professional, suburban. Geographic separation from Seattle's bohemian scene reinforced compositional ambition. Eastside character (precise, structured) maps to progressive metal aesthetic.
theory_nodes.description: The Eastside produces progressive metal. Suburban ambition as creative engine.

theory_nodes.concept: KISW Seattle radio: early supporter of Queensryche EP (1983). Heavy rock radio as part of Northwest metal ecosystem. O'Connor / KISW partial funding of debut EP.
theory_nodes.description: Seattle radio's role in establishing Northwest metal commercial infrastructure.

cross_references.relationship: Metal arc #1 to #2. E=9 crossover velocity to E=8 progressive precision. Oak Harbor island periphery to Bellevue Eastside organization. Combat Records underground to EMI major label.
Metal arc #2 to #3. E=8 progressive to E=7 sludge. Bellevue Eastside to Capitol Hill urban. EMI major label to Sargent House independent.

E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Two great voices in simultaneous performance. Queensryche's Tate five-octave operatic metal voice + Trumpeter Swan (loudest waterfowl) + Sandhill Crane prehistoric bugle. All three: among the most demanding vocal instruments in their respective domains.

milestones: MOST COMMERCIALLY SUCCESSFUL NORTHWEST METAL BAND
